questions.q: Humayun was which emperor in the line of Mughal rulers?
questions.explanation: Humayun was the second Mughal emperor, succeeding his father Babur.

questions.q: Whose son was Humayun?
questions.explanation: Humayun was the son of Babur, the founder of the Mughal Empire.

questions.q: Who drove Humayun out of India, forcing him into exile?
questions.explanation: Humayun lost his throne to the Afghan ruler Sher Shah Suri.

questions.q: In which land did Humayun spend years in exile?
questions.explanation: Humayun spent years in exile at the Safavid court in Persia.

questions.q: In which year did Humayun reclaim Delhi?
questions.explanation: Humayun reclaimed Delhi in 1555 after years of exile.

questions.q: Who succeeded Humayun as Mughal emperor?
questions.explanation: Humayun's young son Akbar succeeded him after his death in 1556.

questions.q: Roughly how soon after reclaiming his throne did Humayun die?
questions.explanation: Humayun died in early 1556, only months after recovering Delhi in 1555.

questions.q: Humayun's tomb is located in which city?
questions.explanation: Humayun's Tomb, an architectural landmark, stands in Delhi and is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

questions.q: The card describes Humayun with which epithet?
questions.explanation: Humayun is described as 'The Fortune-Tossed Emperor' for his turbulent reign of loss and recovery.

questions.q: Which empire did Humayun rule?
questions.explanation: Humayun was the second emperor of the Mughal Empire founded by his father Babur.

questions.q: In which year did Humayun first lose his throne to Sher Shah Suri?
questions.explanation: After defeats at Chausa and Kannauj, Humayun lost his empire to Sher Shah Suri by 1540.

questions.q: At which 1539 battle did Sher Shah defeat Humayun, with many drowning in the Ganges?
questions.explanation: The Battle of Chausa in 1539 was a major defeat for Humayun against Sher Shah.

questions.q: At which 1540 battle did Sher Shah decisively defeat Humayun, driving him from India?
questions.explanation: The Battle of Kannauj in 1540 sealed Humayun's loss of the empire to Sher Shah Suri.

questions.q: Which Safavid Persian ruler gave Humayun refuge during his exile?
questions.explanation: Shah Tahmasp I of Safavid Persia sheltered Humayun and aided his return.

questions.q: Akbar, who succeeded Humayun, was born during his father's exile in which town?
questions.explanation: Akbar was born at Amarkot in Sindh in 1542 while Humayun was a fugitive.

questions.q: How did Humayun die in 1556?
options: Of old age in his sleep
From a fall down the steps of his library
questions.explanation: Humayun died after falling down the stone steps of his library in Delhi.

questions.q: Which loyal noble served as Humayun's chief general and later as regent for the young Akbar?
questions.explanation: Bairam Khan was Humayun's trusted general and became regent for the boy-emperor Akbar.

questions.q: Humayun's Tomb in Delhi is notable as the first major Mughal example of which feature?
options: A garden-tomb with a large dome (charbagh setting)
questions.explanation: Humayun's Tomb pioneered the Mughal garden-tomb (charbagh) form and influenced the Taj Mahal.

questions.q: Conflict with which of his own relatives, including Kamran Mirza, weakened Humayun's position?
questions.explanation: Humayun was undermined by rivalry with his brothers, especially Kamran Mirza, during his struggles.

questions.q: The name 'Humayun' carries which meaning, fitting his eventual fortunes?
questions.explanation: Humayun means 'fortunate' or 'auspicious', ironic given his early misfortunes before his recovery.

questions.q: Roughly how many years did Humayun spend away from his empire before reclaiming Delhi?
questions.explanation: Humayun was out of power from 1540 to 1555, around fifteen years, before his return.

questions.q: Humayun's Tomb in Delhi was commissioned by whom?
questions.explanation: Humayun's Tomb was commissioned by his widow Bega Begum, also called Haji Begum.

questions.q: Which Persian-trained architect is traditionally credited with designing Humayun's Tomb?
questions.explanation: Mirak Mirza Ghiyas is the architect traditionally credited with Humayun's Tomb.

questions.q: During his exile, Humayun briefly took refuge with which ruler before reaching Persia?
options: The ruler of Sindh and then onward
questions.explanation: Humayun wandered through Sindh and the deserts before securing Safavid Persian support.

questions.q: With Persian help, Humayun first recaptured which strongholds before advancing on India?
questions.explanation: Backed by Persia, Humayun seized Kandahar and Kabul before reconquering north India.

questions.q: Humayun's reconquest of Delhi in 1555 was made easier by the disintegration of which dynasty?
options: The Sur dynasty after Sher Shah's death
questions.explanation: The Sur empire fragmented into rival claimants after Sher Shah's death, easing Humayun's return.

questions.q: An intimate Persian-language memoir of Humayun's life was written by his half-sister; what is she called?
questions.explanation: Gulbadan Begum, Humayun's half-sister, wrote the Humayun-nama, a rare first-hand court memoir.

questions.q: Humayun is said to have divided his early administration according to the elements, an unusual scheme reflecting his interest in what?
questions.explanation: Humayun had a noted fascination with astrology, even organising aspects of his court by the elements and planets.

questions.q: The famed Persian painters Mir Sayyid Ali and Abd al-Samad, who later seeded Mughal painting, joined whose service during the exile?
questions.explanation: Humayun recruited Persian masters Mir Sayyid Ali and Abd al-Samad, founding the Mughal painting workshop later expanded under Akbar.

questions.q: Whom did Humayun defeat at the Battle of Sirhind in 1555 to clear his path back to Delhi?
options: The Sur forces of Sikandar Shah Suri
questions.explanation: At Sirhind in 1555, Humayun's forces under Bairam Khan defeated Sikandar Shah Suri, opening the road to Delhi.
